userStory: As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state

This workflow validates: - Initial Home page load in guest state - Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls - Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls - Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view - Navigation from guest-accessible feature buttons to their supported destinations - Absence or non-actionability of authenticated-only controls and feature buttons - Absence of the logged-in user name button while unauthenticated

Launch the application at http://localhost/washtabui/home - Verify the Home page loads in guest state - Verify the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, and approved guest-accessible feature buttons are visible and enabled - Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to the Login page or Login view - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to the customer-search page - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to the vehicle-survey page - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to the survey page - Verify authenticated-only controls and the logged-in user name button are not visible or not actionable while in guest state
Application entry URL: http://localhost/washtabui/home - No additional approved credentials or user data were provided - Guest-state execution should remain unauthenticated throughout the workflow

Home Page Guest-State Elements: - Person/Profile button - Customer signup button - Clean survey button - Site survey button Shared Home Shell Controls: - Back button - Notification button - Home button Navigation Destinations: - Login page or Login view - Customer-search page - Vehicle-survey page - Survey page Ownership Guidance: - All guest-state Home controls belong to the Home page POM/resource - Shared Home shell controls belong to the Home page POM/resource - The Person/Profile button belongs to the Home page POM/resource - The Login page must not own the Person/Profile button because navigation originates from Home

When an unauthenticated user launches the application, the Home page must load in guest state - Guest-state controls and approved guest-accessible feature buttons must be visible and enabled - Selecting the Person/Profile button must open the Login page or Login view - Guest-accessible feature buttons must navigate to their supported destinations - Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons must not be visible or must not be actionable in guest state - The logged-in user name button must not be visible while the user is unauthenticated - The Home page guest state and authenticated state are different states of the same Home page resource

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated and the approved guest-state controls are available on the shared Home page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify all approved guest-state buttons and shared Home shell controls are visible and enabled on Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, and actionable on the Home page guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button navigates from Home guest state to Login page or Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while maintaining unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button navigates from Home guest state to customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The customer-search page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button navigates from Home guest state to vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The vehicle-survey page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button navigates from Home guest state to survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The survey page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening Login view from Person/Profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page guest state and the approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify navigation back to Home guest state after opening survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify repeated clicking of Person/Profile button does not create broken navigation state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Only the Login page or Login view is opened and the application does not display duplicate overlays, broken rendering, or inconsistent navigation behavior.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state workflow navigation
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button remains consistently visible and enabled during guest-state navigation flows.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state workflow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation without exposing authenticated-only functionality.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state workflow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation without changing the user authentication state.

validations: The Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated and the approved guest-state controls are available on the shared Home page.
All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, and actionable on the Home page guest state.
The application transitions from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while maintaining unauthenticated state.
The customer-search page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.
The vehicle-survey page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.
The survey page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated.
Only the Login page or Login view is opened and the application does not display duplicate overlays, broken rendering, or inconsistent navigation behavior.
The Home button remains consistently visible and enabled during guest-state navigation flows.
The Notification button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation without exposing authenticated-only functionality.
The Back button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation without changing the user authentication state.
transitions: The Home page loads successfully in guest state while the user remains unauthenticated and the approved guest-state controls are available on the shared Home page.
All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, and actionable on the Home page guest state.
The application transitions from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while maintaining unauthenticated state.
The customer-search page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.
The vehicle-survey page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.
The survey page opens from the Home guest state and the session remains unauthenticated.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the approved guest-state controls remain visible and enabled without authenticating the user.
The application returns to the Home page guest state and the user remains unauthenticated.
Only the Login page or Login view is opened and the application does not display duplicate overlays, broken rendering, or inconsistent navigation behavior.
The Home button remains consistently visible and enabled during guest-state navigation flows.
The Notification button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation without exposing authenticated-only functionality.
The Back button remains visible and enabled throughout guest-state navigation without changing the user authentication state.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
